insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Saudi Arabia's performance during hydration breaks has been a mixed bag, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight average decline in performance. When Saudi Arabia was pressing before a hydration break, they tended to lose momentum, with their performance declining at an average rate of 0.100 minutes, and they were unable to maintain their momentum in any of these instances. On the other hand, when they weren't pressing before a break, their performance remained relatively stable, with no average decline or gain. Overall, these statistics suggest that hydration breaks can be a challenge for Saudi Arabia, particularly when they're pushing hard before the break, and they may need to adjust their strategy to better maintain their momentum coming out of these stops.
